pi_items.source: Task description and required operations using BlenderBIM/Bonsai.
Task operates on IFC BIM models.
Task involves auditing and repairing spatial hierarchy in an IFC model.
BIM and IFC models are central to architecture and AEC industry.
export_result.sh outputs the results to this exact file.
setup_task.sh generates the flattened model at this exact path.
Task instructions and verifier logic require the repaired model to be saved here.
verifier.py checks for 'ground' and 'first' strings in the output storey names.

pi_items.metadata_value: threshold=80. Anti-gaming: dumping all elements into one storey yields max 75 pts.
pi_items.verified_value: threshold=80. Anti-gaming: dumping all elements into one storey yields max 65 pts.
pi_items.source: verifier.py scoring assigns a maximum of 65 points for one storey (10 file + 15 nomenclature + 15 building flattened fixed + 25 lower elements = 65 points). Additionally, the verifier docstring incorrectly claims 33 elements, while the FZK-Haus model actually contains 96 elements in its spatial structures.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the standard FZK-Haus IFC model, which contains 96 geometric elements in its spatial structures (38 in the lower storey Erdgeschoss, 58 in Dachgeschoss). The setup script correctly flattens this model into '/home/ga/IFCModels/fzk_flat.ifc' by dumping all elements directly into the IfcBuilding root. The agent must create 'Ground' and 'First' storeys and reassign the elements. The target output path is '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_repaired.ifc'. The pass score is 80; dumping all 96 elements into a single storey mathematically yields a maximum of 65 points (if named 'Ground First') or 57 points (if named 'Ground' or 'First'), contradicting the metadata's 75 pts claim.
